parts of the Orlando metro evacuate if their homes are near the rivers: Econ and St. John’s especially. St, John’s area floods a lot. There are a lot of lakes, mostly in Seminole County. Orlando usually gets it worse when it’s an East Coast storm. But, Charley did a number on Orlando.

lifetime Floridian here 54 years more times than not our go to area is in fact Orlando from this coast most of the time we have been comfortable in that decision.

Inland doesn’t evacuate (trailer homes/modular homes excluded) Evacuations are reserved for coastal areas.
If you’re currently on the coast, and plan to evacuate, don’t evacuate to an area inland still in the storms path. That includes Orlando. You’ll still feel affects. Possibly lose power and you’ll be miserable. Evacuate far far south. Or North.
typically, the only parts of Orlando that evacuate are those at risk of freshwater flooding from nearby streams and rivers.
